latest.content:

RNS Number : 1367G
Kropz PLC
29 May 2026
29 May 2026
Kropz Plc
("
Kropz
" or the "
Company
")
Total Voting Rights
Kropz plc (AIM: KRPZ) announces that in compliance with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules that, as at the date of this announcement, it has 1,647,367,866 Ordinary Shares, with each share carrying the right to one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury.
The total voting rights figure is therefore 1,647,367,866 and may be used by shareholders (and others with notification obligations) as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
For further information visit

About Kropz Plc
Kropz is an emerging African phosphate producer and developer with projects in South Africa and in the Republic of Congo. The vision of the Group is to become a leading independent phosphate rock producer and to develop into an integrated, mine-to-market plant nutrient company focusing on sub-Saharan Africa.

Receipt of Exchange Control Approval,
Admission Date of New Ordinary Shares
and
Total Voting Rights
Kropz plc (AIM: KRPZ), an emerging African phosphate producer, is pleased to announce that it has received approval from the South African Reserve Bank ("
Exchange Control Approval
") in relation to Fundraising and Amendment to Loan Terms as announced on 25 February 2026.
Consequently, the Fundraising is now wholly unconditional.
An application has been made for the 79,776,625 new Ordinary Shares ("New Ordinary Shares") pursuant to the Fundraising to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission"). Dealings in respect of the New Ordinary Shares are expected to commence on or around 8.00 a.m. on 6 May 2026.
Following Admission, the Company's issued share capital will be
1,647,367,866 Ordinary Shares. This figure of
1,647,367,866
may then be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Operational Update
latest.content:
Today 08:44
RNS Number : 1144B
Kropz PLC
20 April 2026
20 April 2026
Kropz Plc
("
Kropz
" or the "
Company
")
Operational Update
Kropz Plc (AIM: KRPZ), an emerging African phosphate producer and developer, announces a trading update for the period to 31 March 2026.
Elandsfontein
The quarter and financial period closed with a record production month at Elandsfontein in March 2026, during which 40,792 tonnes of phosphate concentrate were produced.
As previously announced on 24 November 2025, September 2025 marked a production record of 34,070 tonnes. Since that announcement, four additional production records have been achieved during the final six months of the financial period ended 31 March 2026.
Elandsfontein produced 115,686 tonnes of phosphate concentrate in Q4 of the financial period ended 31 March 2026, representing a 11% increase compared to the previous quarter. Sales for the period totalled 104,900 tonnes.
The mine remains in its trial production phase.
Hinda Project
The Hinda Project has performed a review and assessment of alternative production scenarios, which involved comprehensive stress testing and other additional studies. As a result, the complexities associated with both surface and underground water have been significantly reduced and effectively mitigates the risk associated through optimisation initiative. Therefore, management no longer considers water-related challenges to pose a material risk, contributing to improved overall project profitability. This is a major milestone and repositions the entire feasibility of the project.
For further information visit

Drawdown on Bridge Loan Facilities
Kropz Plc (AIM: KRPZ), an emerging African phosphate producer and developer, announced on 8 December 2025 that Kropz Elandsfontein (Pty) Ltd ("
Elandsfontein
") and ARC Fund ("
ARC
") (together the "
Parties
") have agreed to a ZAR 250 million (approximately US$ 14.4 million) loan facility (the "
Loan
")
The third draw down on the Loan for an amount of ZAR 40 million has been made. ZAR 60 million remains available to draw down on the Loan.
As announced on 20 February 2025, following approval from the South African Reserve Bank ("
Exchange Control Approval
") under the South African Exchange Control Regulations, 1961 for the ARC conditional subscription , the Loan balance will be further reduced by approximately ZAR 20 million, resulting in approximately ZAR 40 million remaining to be drawn down on the Loan.

company_30s.what_they_do: KRPZ Ltd. is a mining and resource company that extracts and sells phosphate, a key fertilizer ingredient. They operate a single mine that supplies phosphate to agricultural markets.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Phosphate is a foundational commodity for global food production. Demand grows with population and bio‑fuel development, giving KRPZ a steady, long‑term market. Their mine has a long life plan (≈35 years) and a high free‑float, meaning the company can attract institutional capital if the price turns.
company_30s.current_state: The business is still in a low‑cash‑burn phase, focusing on sustaining operations and maintaining the mine’s production licence. The share price sits at a very low multiple of earnings, trading at a small fraction of its historic valuation, which suggests a steep discount to the underlying asset.

rns_narrative: Over the last 18 months the company has been restructuring its debt and securing short‑term bridge loans to keep the mine running. The latest filing confirms a new ECA approval and an admission date for shares, signalling that the company is actively working to raise fresh capital.

A second thread is the gradual reduction of loan terms and the release of a 2026 operational update, which shows that production volumes are stabilising and that the company is meeting its mine‑life targets.
Together these events paint a picture of a company that is still struggling but has managed to stay afloat and is now positioning itself for a potential turnaround if a catalyst arrives.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet survival – the community is largely silent, with only a handful of bullish chatter about a possible rebound.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders believe the latest capital raise will finally give the mine enough liquidity to expand output. They see the share price as a cheap entry point for a long‑term play.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most skeptics point to the repeated debt restructuring and the lack of a clear, revenue‑generating catalyst. They worry that the company may never lift the debt load and could face a forced sale or delisting.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat. The few bullish posts are recent and tied to the new capital‑raising announcement, but overall engagement remains low, indicating that the market has largely ignored KRPZ.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent interest. The lack of search activity means any new announcement could create a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The scheduled 2026 operational update – if it reports a 10‑15% lift in output or a new supply contract, it could act as a buying signal.
A successful funding round that raises at least £10 m would reduce the debt burden and could be interpreted as a vote of confidence.
An unexpected drop in phosphate prices could lower operating costs and improve margins, making the mine more profitable.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor the share price for a break of the 52‑week low support. A spike in trading volume that exceeds 2‑3× the average daily volume would suggest capitulation is complete and a mean‑reversion could start.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep crash. The APEX score (8/21) indicates tight compression, which is a classic pre‑bounce setup. Confirmation will come if the price holds above the recent low for at least 10 trading days.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: KRPZ’s signal is a GREEN deep‑crash bottom, historically yielding a 39% average return. If the price recovers to 60% of its pre‑crash peak, the upside is roughly 70% from the current level. The mean‑reversion thesis is strong because the company has a long‑term asset and the market has ignored it, creating a high surprise potential.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still carries a large bridge‑loan debt and has a history of restructuring. If the next funding round fails or the operational update shows flat or declining output, the price could stay stuck. The worst‑case scenario is a 30% further decline before any recovery, leaving the share price near its current valuation.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~70% (from current level to 60% of pre‑crash peak). Potential downside: ~30% (if the price falls further). Risk‑reward ratio: about 2.3:1 – a classic asymmetric trade that favors the contrarian.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 50‑day moving average and volume is 2× average. Size the position so that a 10% drop would wipe out a single trade (tight stop at the 52‑week low).
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear operational update that shows a 10% output lift or a fresh capital raise. A volume spike of 3× average with a price break above the recent high would be a strong entry trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risk has surfaced. The company’s asset base and long‑term plan keep it afloat.

ai_insights.bottom_line: KRPZ sits at a deep crash, is quietly ignored by the market, and could bounce back to 60% of its pre‑crash level once a modest operational or funding catalyst materialises, offering a 2‑3× reward for a 10‑30% risk.
